title: C4OS Grill Question 002c: Browser Safety Floor
description: This follow-up resolves a contradiction in the Browser answer. Submit the form, then paste the generated response back into chat.

questions.label: Which Browser safety position should govern MVP?
options: Browser stays in MVP only with hard isolation: no privileged bridge, no credentials, trusted-root or generated-artifact file policy, no shell state, downloads disabled
Browser stays in MVP with arbitrary file:// and any-page automation, accepting that this is not a trusted-root-safe design
Browser is deferred until full native Browser isolation can be proven
Split Browser into two MVP surfaces: user Browser with restricted file policy, and agent preview/automation only for approved localhost or generated artifacts
questions.help: Recommended answer: split Browser into user Browser and agent preview/automation surfaces. Do not allow arbitrary file:// navigation or any-page automation in the same trust context.

questions.label: Revise the file:// policy
options: Only generated artifacts and trusted-root files opened by explicit user action
Only trusted-root files, whether user or agent navigated
Any local file the user explicitly chooses through an open-file dialog
Any file URL the page or agent tries to navigate to
questions.help: Recommended answer: generated artifacts and trusted-root files by explicit user action. Page-originated arbitrary file navigation should be blocked.

questions.label: Revise the agent Browser automation policy
options: Agent automation only for approved localhost previews and generated artifacts
Agent automation for any page, but only after user approval per domain
Agent automation for any page without additional approval
No agent Browser automation in MVP
questions.help: Recommended answer: approved localhost previews and generated artifacts only. General web automation is a separate product surface.

questions.label: Revise cookies/login persistence
options: No persistence in MVP; ephemeral per Browser session
Mode split: ephemeral for preview/automation, persistent for user browsing only
questions.help: Recommended answer: mode split if full user browsing is truly MVP; otherwise ephemeral per session.

questions.label: Clarifications or non-negotiables
questions.help: State anything you refuse to constrain. If you keep arbitrary file:// or any-page automation, explain what user value justifies the risk.
